title: Triage the auth log
prompt: First question at every incident: how bad is it? auth.log is a realistic SSH log — Accepted and Failed lines mixed together. Count how many lines contain 'Failed password' and write just that number into answer.txt. grep -c counts matching lines directly (no pipe needed — though grep | wc -l works too).

details.restate: answer.txt holds one number: how many lines of auth.log contain the exact phrase 'Failed password'. The Accepted lines don't count.

io.because: -c makes grep print the number of matching lines instead of the lines

terms.meaning: the log where Linux records every login attempt — the first file an analyst opens

help.plain: grep can count its own matches — or pipe the matches into a line counter, like the pipes mission.
help.analogy: A turnstile counter at the door: you don't watch the crowd, you read the tally.
help.nudge: You counted ERROR lines once before with a pipe. grep also has a flag that skips the pipe entirely.
